raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to cd to the alphabetically last path under /workspace. That means list directories under /workspace, sort, pick last, cd into it. Use cd "$(ls -1 /workspace | sort | tail -n1)" but need to handle spaces. Use find? But simple: cd "$(ls -A1 /workspace | sort | tail -n1)". If there are files too? "path" maybe directories. Use find -maxdepth 1 -type d. So: cd "$(find /workspace -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | sort | tail -n1)". That's good. Provide command.

Use find -maxdepth 1 -type d. So: cd "$(find /workspace -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | sort | tail -n1)". That's good. Provide command.
